data.content:
# Implementation Review — REJECTED

## Verdict
Rejecting and rolling back to ready_for_implementation. The implementation step
(2026-05-04T11:47:09 → 11:47:14, 5s, Codex 5.5) made no code change and the
acceptance criteria for `kno ls --json` are NOT met.

## Evidence

Empirical (against the locally installed kno 0.15.7 binary):

- `kno show knots-e935 --json | jq .lease_agent` returns populated provider,
  agent_name, agent_type, model, model_version. ✅ AC #1 met.
- `kno ls --json | jq '.data[0].lease_agent'` returns `null` for the same
  active knot (knots-e935, lease_id=knots-a08d, state=implementation_review).
  ❌ AC #2 NOT met.

Source confirms the gap, not just an artifact of the binary build:

- src/app/types.rs:171-213 — `KnotView::from(KnotCacheRecord)` always sets
  `lease_agent: None`. The field is `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`,
  so when it stays None it is omitted from JSON entirely.
- src/app/query.rs:35-62 — `show_knot` calls `enrich_bound_lease_agent`
  (line 41) before returning, which is why `kno show` works.
- src/app/query.rs:21-33 — `list_knots` does NOT call
  `enrich_bound_lease_agent`. Every KnotView produced by listing therefore
  has `lease_agent = None`. This is the bug.

## Required fix

In `src/app/query.rs::list_knots`, after building each `KnotView`, call
`self.enrich_bound_lease_agent(&mut knot)?` for each knot in the result
(or batch-resolve lease records to avoid N+1 lookups). Mirror the existing
behavior in `show_knot`.

## Tests required (AC #3)

Add a regression test that:
1. Creates an action knot, creates a lease with known agent identity, claims
   the knot with that lease.
2. Calls `app.list_knots()` and asserts `lease_agent` is populated with the
   expected provider/agent/model/version on the active knot.
3. Asserts a queued knot with no lease yields `lease_agent: None`
   (distinguishing absence from a real "unknown" value).

## Other notes

- No branch and no commits exist for this knot — `git log --all --since="2026-05-03"`
  shows no implementation commits and the working tree is clean. The
  implementation step appears to have been a no-op.
- AC #1 (kno show) is satisfied by code that already existed before this knot
  was claimed; the new work needed is the listing path.
